description:
Migrate from VMware Fusion to a unified devbox generation system supporting:
- **UTM** (macOS hosts) — replaces VMware Fusion, supports nested virtualization
- **KVM/libvirt** (NixOS hosts) — same devbox workflow on NixOS machines
- **macOS guests** — virtualize macOS in addition to NixOS Linux guests

## Current State
- `vm-generate` devshell command builds VMware images via `nixosConfigurations.*.config.system.build.vmwareImage`
- `fusion` feature flag gates VMware guest tools and image generation
- Two devboxes exist: `ash-devbox` and `spectre-devbox` (aarch64-linux NixOS VMs)
- `nixos-generators` is already a flake input (unused currently for this)

## Target State
- `devbox-generate <hostname>` command that auto-detects hypervisor from host/devbox config
- Host type extended with `devbox.hypervisor` (utm | kvm | vmware) and `devbox.guest` (linux | macos)
- UTM: generates qcow2 images, optionally auto-imports via `utmctl`
- KVM: generates qcow2 images for libvirt on NixOS hosts
- macOS guests: generates UTM-compatible macOS VM configs (IPSW-based)
- Feature flag migration: `fusion` → `devbox` (with hypervisor sub-option)

## Dependency Graph
```mermaid
graph LR
    A[nix-TASK1: devbox type system] --> D[nix-TASK4: UTM guest modules]
    A --> E[nix-TASK5: KVM guest modules]
    A --> F[nix-TASK6: macOS guest support]
    B[nix-TASK2: image generation infra] --> G[nix-TASK7: devbox-generate command]
    D --> G
    E --> G
    C[nix-TASK3: migrate fusion → devbox] --> D
    C --> E
    F --> G
```
title: Devbox generation: multi-hypervisor, multi-platform
